feature_name: v1.16.0 "Watch experience" -- one cohesive round of 5 items around "keep watching / keep your place" (mini-player PiP, prev/next, autoplay-next, persist home-view, share-URL validator robustness)
current_tasks_note: ACCEPTANCE COMPLETE (2026-07-06). All 5 tasks (T1-T5) implemented, build green 1120/1120 on Node 22, lint clean, two-reviewer gate cleared (8 findings + 1 release-blocking router-ordering bug found and remediated). product-manager verified all 37 ACs against the code + test suite: 16 UNIT/INTEGRATION/PROCESS criteria PASS outright; 21 MANUAL criteria are code-verified with Dean's on-device pass pending as the documented arbiter for playback/mobile/navigation feel (by design, not a gap). Verdict: ACCEPT-WITH-NOTES -- see the exec plan's new '## Acceptance' section for the full per-FR breakdown. Recommend advancing to Done once Dean's on-device pass confirms the MANUAL items, per the ship cadence already on record (merge -> tag v1.16.0 -> Docker publish -> Dean's on-device pass).
base: main. NOTE: the working tree is currently on branch feat/icon-sets; the coordinator owns ALL git and will create feature/v1.16-miniplayer off main before implementation. Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (v1.10 audio-art+related/rankRelated, v1.12 one-off download, v1.13 polish, v1.14 quickwins, v1.15 bigswing).
git_ownership_note: Coordinator owns ALL git. SDEs must NOT commit/stage/push. SDE reports files-changed + test output; coordinator commits per task.
node_toolchain_note: Node 22 is the toolchain standard. A Node 22 toolchain is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin -- build-verify MUST run tests on Node 22. fnm PATH export still needed for the default: export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH".
process_plan: FULL PIPELINE, RIGHT-SIZED per item. HEAVIER review (careful QA + adversarial finder / two-reviewer gate) reserved for item 5 (lib/ytdlp/url.js -- the yt-dlp spawn security guard; any change is scrutinized so extraction can NOT smuggle a bad URL past any existing check). LIGHTER QA (confirm no-regression only) for items 1-4, which are UX/visual -- Dean's ON-DEVICE pass is the ARBITER for all playback/mobile UX (esp. iOS PiP behavior, mobile autoplay-next, back-navigation scroll restore). Ship cadence: merge -> tag v1.16.0 -> Docker publish (CI is the image arbiter) -> Dean's on-device pass.
hard_constraints: ADDITIVE / NO REGRESSIONS. Every item layers on top of existing watch/home behavior; the existing resume overlay, skip-controls, audio visualizer, related sidebar, transcode overlay, mobile-autoplay-disabled posture, and home sort/sidebar/search must keep working unchanged.

OPTIONAL yt-dlp MODULE DISABLED-PATH NO-OP IS SACRED (item 5). lib/ytdlp/url.js only executes on the yt-dlp path; when FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED is off the module is byte-identical no-op. Item 5 must not add routes/UI or change any disabled-path behavior -- it only normalizes the INPUT to the existing validator.

ITEM 5 SECURITY IS NON-NEGOTIABLE: the existing strict guards in validateChannelUrl do NOT change -- FORBIDDEN_CHARS (url.js:39/:143), MAX_URL_LENGTH (:30/:129), leading-'-' reject (:137), new URL() parse (:148), http/https-only (:154), userinfo reject (:166), ALLOWED_HOSTS allowlist (:19/:171), isPlausiblePath shape check (:106/:175), and SF5 decoded ?v=/?list= id charset+length (:63/:66). ONLY the input is normalized BEFORE those checks: coerce-to-string + trim, then if internal whitespace remains extract the first /https?:\/\/\S+/ run as the candidate, then run the UNCHANGED strict validation on that candidate. Extraction must NOT be able to smuggle a hostile URL past any check.

follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes; prefer textContent over innerHTML for NEW dynamic strings (XSS-safe -- esp. any new watch-page control labels/status); node:test for new/regression tests; lint 0 warnings; NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties / --vars). Any new CSS (the PiP button, prev/next controls, an autoplay-next toggle surface) must use existing theme tokens; no hardcoded colors that break the 2005/2009/2014/2021 eras or light/dark modes. Feature-detect PiP and show the control only when supported.

iOS reality (items 1 & 3): iOS Safari uses the webkit presentation-mode API (video.webkitSupportsPresentationMode('picture-in-picture') / video.webkitSetPresentationMode('picture-in-picture')), NOT the standard requestPictureInPicture(); the standard 'enterpictureinpicture'/'leavepictureinpicture' events + document.pictureInPictureEnabled feature-detect are the desktop/Android-Chromium path. Autoplay-next on the <video> 'ended' event rides an existing user-gesture chain (usually allowed) but note any iOS limitation; do NOT re-enable general mobile autoplay (watch.js:684 keeps desktop-only autoplay for the initial load).

product_forks_for_dean.item_2_playlist_order_source: RECOMMEND the derived playlist = the CURRENT HOME SORT ORDER (newest/oldest/title/size/random, persisted in localStorage 'filetube_sort', applied by sortItems in public/js/main.js). Prev/next step through that ordered list. Alternative: use the rankRelated (v1.10.0, common.js:373) ranking. Home-sort order is the more predictable 'up next' for a user who clicked from the home grid and matches the mental model of a playlist. PE designs HOW the order + current position reach the watch page (recommend: re-derive the same order on the watch page from GET /api/videos + the persisted sort, so a deep-link/refresh still works, rather than only passing an id-list via URL params which breaks on refresh). PM confirms the source; PE owns the delivery mechanism.
product_forks_for_dean.item_2_ends_wrap_vs_disable: RECOMMEND DISABLE prev at the start / next at the end (greyed-out, non-wrapping). Wrapping around from last->first is surprising for a finite local library and complicates autoplay-next (item 3) termination. Disable-at-ends is the simpler, clearer default. PM confirms.
product_forks_for_dean.item_3_autoplay_pref_storage: RECOMMEND storing the autoplay-next toggle as a SERVER setting in db.settings, defaultView-style (add 'autoplayNext' to the KNOWN_KEYS allowlist at server.js:1718 + settingsResponse at :1693 + withDefaultSettings default false). This makes it a real cross-device Setting like the others on the Settings page. Alternative: a client-only localStorage pref matching 'filetube_sort'/theme prefs (simpler, no server change, but per-device). Server-setting is the more 'first-class Setting' choice and matches the item's framing ('persisted like defaultView'); PM confirms server-setting vs client-pref. OFF by default either way.
product_forks_for_dean.item_4_restore_mechanism: RECOMMEND sessionStorage: on navigate-away from home (click into a video / any in-app navigation), save { folder/search filter, sort, scrollY } under a session key; on home load, if that key is present (i.e. we returned from within the app), restore the exact view + scroll, then clear/refresh it. Do NOT restore on a fresh tab / deep-link / external entry (only when returning from within the app). Alternatives PE weighs: history.state, and/or bfcache-friendly handling (pageshow event) as a complement. sessionStorage is the most reliable across the multi-page (watch.html -> index.html) navigation. PM confirms the exact 'what to preserve' set (folder + search + sort + scroll).
product_forks_for_dean.item_5_extract_vs_bare: RECOMMEND EXTRACT-then-strictly-validate: trim first; if internal whitespace remains, extract the first /https?:\/\/\S+/ substring as the candidate and run it through the UNCHANGED strict validation. Alternative: require a bare URL and reject anything with surrounding text (rejects the real-world YouTube share-sheet payload 'Title\nhttps://youtu.be/<id>?si=<x>' -- the whole point of this item). Extract-then-validate accepts the share-sheet reality WITHOUT weakening any guard (a hostile embedded URL like 'click https://evil.com/x' extracts to evil.com and still fails the host allowlist). PM confirms; the security tests below are MANDATED regardless.

1.title: Mini-player via native Picture-in-Picture (cross-browser + iOS webkit fallback)
1.review_weight: LIGHTER QA (no-regression) -- Dean on-device (esp. iOS PiP) is the ARBITER
1.current_behavior_confirmed: watch.html:123 has <video id="media-player" controls preload="metadata" playsinline webkit-playsinline x5-playsinline>. No PiP control today. Player setup lives in public/js/watch.js. Native PiP floats an OS-level video window that survives navigation automatically -- NO in-app docked element / NO SPA refactor (Dean-LOCKED: native PiP, not a docked in-app player).
1.fix: Add a PiP toggle/button on the watch-page player, shown ONLY when PiP is supported (feature-detect). Use the standard Web API (video.requestPictureInPicture() / document.exitPictureInPicture(), 'enterpictureinpicture'/'leavepictureinpicture' events, document.pictureInPictureEnabled) with the iOS Safari fallback (video.webkitSupportsPresentationMode('picture-in-picture') / video.webkitSetPresentationMode('picture-in-picture')). Feature-detect both; hide the control when neither is supported. Reflect PiP state on the button; do not break the existing playsinline behavior.
files: public/watch.html (PiP button markup in the player controls area)
public/js/watch.js (feature-detection, requestPictureInPicture/webkit fallback, event wiring, button state)
1.arbiter: Dean on-device -- iOS Safari PiP + desktop/Android Chromium PiP; no-regression QA on the existing player

2.title: Prev/next video navigation (derived playlist order) -- FOUNDATION for item 3
2.current_behavior_confirmed: The watch page loads related via rankRelated (public/js/common.js:373; watch.js:766). Home sort persists in localStorage 'filetube_sort' (public/js/main.js:18), options newest/oldest/title/size/random via sortItems. GET /api/videos returns the full library. There is NO prev/next control today; the watch page does not know its position in any ordered list.
2.fix: Add prev + next controls on the watch page that step to the previous/next media in a derived ordered 'playlist'. RECOMMEND deriving from the current home sort order (see fork). PE designs HOW the ordered list + current position reach the watch page (recommend re-derive the same order on the watch page from GET /api/videos + the persisted sort, so refresh/deep-link still works; a passed id-list via URL param is an alternative but breaks on refresh). Disable prev-at-start / next-at-end (recommend; see fork). Ties into rankRelated as an alternative order source.
files: public/js/watch.js (derive order, compute position, wire prev/next navigation to /watch.html?v=<id>)
public/js/common.js (if the order-derivation helper is shared with main.js sortItems)
2.arbiter: Dean on-device (prev/next stepping matches the home order) + unit test on the order-derivation/position helper if extracted

3.title: Autoplay next (Settings toggle, OFF by default)
3.review_weight: LIGHTER QA (no-regression) -- Dean on-device arbiter (esp. mobile 'ended' autoplay)
3.current_behavior_confirmed: The <video> 'ended' event already has listeners (watch.js:376, :386 -> setPlaybackState). The player disables autoplay on mobile for the INITIAL load (watch.js:684-689, desktop-only autoplay). Settings persist server-side via db.settings + the KNOWN_KEYS allowlist (server.js:1718) + settingsResponse (:1693) + withDefaultSettings, mirroring defaultView; the Settings page (setup.html) is the UI surface.
3.fix: A persisted toggle (recommend db.settings 'autoplayNext', defaultView-style -- see fork) that, when ON, auto-plays the NEXT media (per item 2's derived order) when the current one ENDS (the 'ended' event). OFF by default. Autoplay-next on 'ended' rides an existing user-gesture chain (usually allowed even on mobile); note any iOS limitation but do NOT re-enable general mobile autoplay for the initial load. When at the end of the list (item 2 disables next), do nothing. DEPENDS ON item 2 (needs the derived next).
files: server.js (db.settings 'autoplayNext' in KNOWN_KEYS + settingsResponse + withDefaultSettings default false) IF server-setting chosen
public/setup.html + its client (the Settings toggle UI) IF server-setting
public/js/watch.js ('ended' handler navigates to next when the pref is ON)
test/unit or test/integration (settings round-trip for the new key)
3.arbiter: Dean on-device (autoplay-next fires on end; OFF is respected; mobile behavior) + settings round-trip test

4.title: Persist home-view state on back-navigation (view + scroll)
4.review_weight: LIGHTER QA (no-regression) -- Dean on-device arbiter (scroll restore)
4.current_behavior_confirmed: FileTube is MULTI-PAGE (watch.html -> index.html; not an SPA). Returning home (logo/back) is a hard load that jumps to the top. public/js/main.js persists ONLY the sort ('filetube_sort'); the active folder filter, search query, and scroll position are lost on navigation.
4.fix: Preserve the prior home view (active folder/search/sort) + scroll position when returning to home from within the app, instead of a top-jumping reload. RECOMMEND sessionStorage: save { folder/search filter, sort, scrollY } on navigate-away; restore the exact view + scroll on home load ONLY when returning from within the app (do NOT restore on a fresh/deep-link/external home load). PE weighs sessionStorage vs history.state vs bfcache/pageshow handling. Must not break a fresh home load.
files: public/js/main.js (save view+scroll on navigate-away; restore on load when a session marker is present)
public/js/common.js (if a shared navigate-away hook is warranted for the logo/back links across pages)
4.arbiter: Dean on-device (back from a video restores folder+search+sort+scroll; fresh load is unaffected)

5.title: Share-URL validator robustness (yt-dlp) -- SECURITY-SENSITIVE, HEAVIER review
5.review_weight: HEAVIER (two-reviewer gate: careful QA + adversarial finder) -- this is the yt-dlp spawn guard
5.current_behavior_confirmed: lib/ytdlp/url.js validateChannelUrl (:125) rejects the real-world YouTube share-sheet payload at the RAW-string FORBIDDEN_CHARS whitespace check (:143) -- e.g. 'Title\nhttps://youtu.be/<id>?si=<token>' fails on the newline/surrounding text. Tracking params (?si=, &feature=share, &pp=) themselves already pass (videoId comes from the path/?v=; buildWatchUrl (:98) rebuilds a clean canonical watch URL dropping ?si). classifySingleVideo (:199) is the one-off single-video classifier. All strict guards (FORBIDDEN_CHARS, MAX_URL_LENGTH, leading-'-', parse, protocol, userinfo, ALLOWED_HOSTS, isPlausiblePath, SF5 id charset) are enumerated in hard_constraints.
5.fix: Make validateChannelUrl robust WITHOUT weakening security: (1) coerce to string + TRIM leading/trailing whitespace first; (2) if the trimmed input STILL contains internal whitespace (text with an embedded URL), EXTRACT the first /https?:\/\/\S+/ substring as the candidate; (3) run the extracted/trimmed candidate through the EXISTING strict validation UNCHANGED (FORBIDDEN_CHARS now runs on a whitespace-free candidate; all other guards identical). Confirm ?si=/&feature=/&pp= still pass and buildWatchUrl still emits a clean canonical URL (no ?si). PE decides exactly where normalization sits (a pre-step inside validateChannelUrl) so both the add-subscription path AND classifySingleVideo inherit it via the single source of truth.
mandated_security_regression_tests: Hostile embedded URL STILL rejected: 'click https://evil.com/x' -> extracted evil.com FAILS the host allowlist.
Extracted URL with a shell metachar / leading '-' / userinfo -> STILL rejected.
A channel/playlist/handle for the one-off single-video path -> STILL 400 (classifySingleVideo kind channel/playlist).
classifySingleVideo returns kind 'video' + the right videoId + a clean buildWatchUrl (no ?si).
files: lib/ytdlp/url.js (input-normalization pre-step in validateChannelUrl; strict guards UNCHANGED)
test/unit ytdlp url tests (the mandated security-regression + acceptance cases)
5.arbiter: two-reviewer gate (careful QA + adversarial finder scrutinizing that extraction can't bypass any check) + unit tests green on Node 22

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. Items needing PE design: 1 (PiP cross-browser + iOS webkit fallback + feature-detection + control), 2 (prev/next derived-order source + how order/position reach the watch page + ends behavior), 4 (persist-state mechanism: sessionStorage vs history.state vs bfcache + what-to-preserve), 5 (URL-extraction pre-step placement, keeping every strict guard unchanged). Item 3 (autoplay-next) is more DIRECT (pref storage + 'ended' handler) but rides through Design for one cohesive plan and because it DEPENDS on item 2's derived-next foundation.
cross_cutting: Items 2 and 3 are a chain: item 2 (prev/next derived order) is the FOUNDATION item 3 (autoplay-next) builds on -- both must agree on the SAME derived order + position + ends behavior, ideally via a shared order-derivation helper. Items 1 and 3 both touch the <video> element and mobile/iOS playback constraints -- keep PiP state and autoplay-next from interfering. Item 4 (persist home view) and item 2 (order derived from home sort) both read the home sort ('filetube_sort') -- keep the source of truth single. Item 5 is isolated to lib/ytdlp/url.js (server-side, yt-dlp path only) and shares nothing with the client watch/home work. NO new deps; the yt-dlp module disabled = byte-identical no-op holds; era-theme CSS vars for all new controls; textContent-not-innerHTML for new dynamic strings; node:test; lint 0.

tasks.title: Persistent shell + SPA-lite router + view-lifecycle refactor (FR-1 core)
tasks.description: Add #view-root/#player-dock/#player-host-template to all four shells (outside #view-root). Build the vanilla SPA-lite router + view registry in common.js (registerView, delegated-click interception for the four known routes, navigate(url,{replace}), popstate, per-view history.state scroll bookkeeping, fetch->DOMParser->extract-#view-root(+title)->swap without appending fetched <script> tags, progressive-enhancement boot calling the same init as a swap). Convert main.js, watch.js, and the extracted setup.js into registered init(root)/destroy() view modules with one per-view AbortController; lazy-load subscriptions.js only on the first /subscriptions nav. Hoist the static player markup into #player-host-template and land the reparent slots/hooks (the dock state machine itself is T2).
acs: FR-1: progressive-enhancement deep-link/full-load of every main view
FR-1: no new scroll-jump/flash beyond design
Cross-cutting: disabled-module no subscriptions swappable entry; no new deps; Node 22 / lint 0 / node:test
tasks.done_when: All four shells carry #view-root/#player-dock/#player-host-template; router intercepts in-app nav for the four routes and swaps #view-root without a full reload; back/forward work; every view URL still full-loads correctly (progressive enhancement); main/watch/setup are registered init/destroy modules with AbortController teardown; subscriptions.js lazy-loads only when enabled; extracted helpers have unit tests; lint 0; tests green on Node 22.

tasks.title: Persistent player controller + docked mini-player + feature preservation (FR-1)
tasks.description: Create public/js/player.js as the persistent controller that clones #player-host-template once into one live #player-host and owns the <video> and all player features for the life of the shell. Implement FULL<->DOCKED<->CLOSED state machine via reparent(appendChild) between inline #player-slot and fixed #player-dock; iOS resume-guard (reparent inside the triggering user gesture + play re-assert with retained currentTime); dock tap-to-expand via idempotent load(id,data,{adopt:true}) with no restart when currentId matches; dock [x] close (pause, clear src+load(), tear down player-scoped timers/listeners). Migrate the player half of watch.js (skip controls, rotate-to-fullscreen, transcode overlay + pollTranscodeUntilReady, resume overlay, audio-bg-art, Media Session + visibilitychange re-assert, progress saver, live-transcode); FULL-only shortcuts active only in FULL. CSS for docked player/#player-dock/#player-slot via era tokens. Keep fixed-overlay fallback documented in comments.
acs: FR-1: dock-on-leave; tap-to-expand same position no reload; [x] close teardown (no orphaned video); full-size no-regression; docked player persists/plays on setup+subscriptions
FR-1: all existing player features preserved in BOTH states (inline iOS playback, +-15s skip, audio-bg-art, transcode overlay+polling, Media Session)
Cross-cutting: era-token CSS; textContent for new dynamic strings
tasks.done_when: One live #player-host survives every in-app nav; FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED transitions work; leaving watch while playing docks and keeps playing; tapping the dock expands to the same media/position with no reload; [x] stops and tears down; every existing player feature works in both states; new CSS uses era tokens; lint 0; tests green on Node 22. Dean's on-device pass (esp. iOS reparent) is the arbiter.

tasks.title: FR-2 prev/next + FR-3 autoplay-next
tasks.description: Add shared unit-tested helpers deriveOrderedIds(items,sort) (wrapping sortItems) and computeNeighbors(orderedIds,currentId)->{prevId,nextId} (null at ends) in common.js. Add watch-view prev/next controls; on watch-view init re-derive order from GET /api/videos + persisted filetube_sort (durable on refresh/deep-link); disable prev at start / next at end (no wrap); optional in-memory navContext to scope to the active home filter with full-library fallback. Add autoplayNext server setting to server.js (DEFAULT_SETTINGS, settingsResponse, KNOWN_KEYS, typeof body.autoplayNext !== 'boolean' 400 branch, mirroring defaultView) and the Settings toggle in setup.js/setup.html. Wire the controller's 'ended' handler: when autoplayNext on and computeNeighbors yields nextId, router.navigate('/watch.html?v='+nextId); at end (nextId null) do nothing; .play() reject on iOS caught (leaves paused). Do NOT re-enable general mobile initial-load autoplay. CSS via era tokens.
acs: FR-2: controls match home order; disable at ends; refresh/deep-link recomputes; helper [UNIT]
FR-3: setting round-trips OFF-by-default [INTEGRATION]; ON navigates to next on 'ended'; OFF unchanged; end-of-order no-op; no mobile initial-autoplay regression
tasks.done_when: Prev/next step through the home sort order and disable at the ends; refresh recomputes position; deriveOrderedIds/computeNeighbors have unit tests; autoplayNext round-trips via /api/settings (OFF default) with an integration test; autoplay-next fires on 'ended' when ON, no-ops at end and when OFF; the 'ended' handler reuses the SAME neighbor logic; lint 0; tests green on Node 22.

tasks.title: FR-4 home-state/scroll retention + navigation polish
tasks.description: Add a single-entry home viewCache hook on the router: on navigate-away from home, detach and retain the (timer-free, no document-level listener) home #view-root node recording scrollY; on return via back/forward or in-app nav to the same home URL, re-attach the cached node instantly (exact grid incl. any rolled random shuffle, filter, sort, scroll; no re-fetch, no re-render flash, no image-height scroll-restore race). A fresh/deep-link/hard load or a different home filter URL builds clean and starts at top. Only the home view is cached. No-flash/no-layout-shift pass across back/forward.
acs: FR-4: in-app return restores folder/search/sort/scroll (no top-jump)
FR-1: no new scroll-jump/flash/layout-shift beyond design
tasks.done_when: Returning to home in-app restores the exact folder/search/sort + scroll with no top-jump or flash; a fresh/deep-link/hard load always starts clean; only the home view is cached; lint 0; tests green on Node 22. May run alongside T3 once T1 lands.

tasks.title: FR-5 validateChannelUrl robustness + the five mandated security tests
tasks.description: Insert an input-normalization pre-step inside validateChannelUrl keeping every strict guard byte-identical and still applied to the candidate: (1) existing non-empty-string guard first; (2) raw.length > MAX_URL_LENGTH reject stays on the ORIGINAL raw string before any regex work; (3) const trimmed = raw.trim(); (4) if /\s/.test(trimmed) extract the first /https?:\/\/\S+/ match as candidate (no match -> keep trimmed, fails FORBIDDEN_CHARS as today), else candidate = trimmed; (5) run the UNCHANGED strict validation on candidate (leading-'-', FORBIDDEN_CHARS, new URL() parse, http/https-only, userinfo, ALLOWED_HOSTS, isPlausiblePath, SF5 id charset/length). classifySingleVideo inherits the normalization (single source of truth). NO new routes/UI; NO disabled-path change; disabled-module no-op byte-identical.
acs: FR-5: hostile embedded URL rejected; extracted metachar/leading-'-'/userinfo rejected; oversized rejected; channel/playlist/handle on single-video path still 400; two ACCEPT share-sheet cases; classifySingleVideo returns kind 'video' + right videoId + clean buildWatchUrl (no ?si)
PROCESS: two-reviewer gate; unit tests green on Node 22
tasks.done_when: validateChannelUrl accepts the trimmed/extracted share-sheet payload while every strict guard is unchanged and still applied to the candidate; the length cap stays on the raw input; all seven mandated FR-5 unit cases pass; classifySingleVideo inherits the normalization; disabled-module path is byte-identical no-op; lint 0; tests green on Node 22.

history.note: New feature round v1.16.0 'Watch experience' initiated by Dean as ONE cohesive round of 5 items around 'keep watching / keep your place'. Target branch feature/v1.16-miniplayer off main (coordinator-owned git; working tree currently on feat/icon-sets). ITEMS: (1) mini-player via NATIVE Picture-in-Picture -- Dean-LOCKED to native PiP (standard requestPictureInPicture + iOS webkit presentation-mode fallback), NOT a docked in-app player / no SPA refactor; PiP button feature-detected on the watch player. (2) prev/next video navigation over a derived playlist order (the home sort order or rankRelated) -- FOUNDATION for item 3. (3) autoplay-next Settings toggle (persisted like defaultView), OFF by default, fires on the <video> 'ended' event, respects mobile-autoplay constraints. (4) persist home-view state (folder/search/sort + scroll) on back-navigation from watch.html -> index.html, restore only when returning from within the app. (5) share-URL validator robustness in lib/ytdlp/url.js -- SECURITY-SENSITIVE: trim + extract-first-URL BEFORE the UNCHANGED strict validation so the YouTube share-sheet payload ('Title\nhttps://youtu.be/<id>?si=<x>') validates without weakening any spawn guard. PROCESS: full pipeline; HEAVIER two-reviewer review on item 5 (yt-dlp spawn guard); LIGHTER no-regression QA on items 1-4 with Dean's on-device pass (esp. iOS PiP) as the arbiter. Ship: merge -> tag v1.16.0 -> Docker publish -> Dean's on-device pass. Reset state from the prior v1.15 record to this v1.16 feature.

EM VERIFIED current code against all 5 items before routing: (1) watch.html:123 <video id=media-player playsinline webkit-playsinline> with player setup in public/js/watch.js and NO PiP control today -- native PiP floats an OS window that survives navigation (no docked element / no SPA work). (2) rankRelated in public/js/common.js:373 (used at watch.js:766); home sort persists in localStorage 'filetube_sort' (public/js/main.js:18) via sortItems (newest/oldest/title/size/random); GET /api/videos returns the library; no prev/next + no position awareness today. (3) 'ended' listeners exist (watch.js:376,:386); mobile autoplay is disabled for the INITIAL load (watch.js:684-689, desktop-only); server settings persist via db.settings + KNOWN_KEYS allowlist (server.js:1718) + settingsResponse (:1693), mirroring defaultView. (4) multi-page (watch.html->index.html); main.js persists ONLY sort, not folder/search/scroll -> hard load jumps to top. (5) lib/ytdlp/url.js validateChannelUrl (:125) rejects the share-sheet payload at the raw FORBIDDEN_CHARS whitespace check (:143); tracking params ?si=/&feature=/&pp= already pass; buildWatchUrl (:98) already rebuilds a clean canonical URL; classifySingleVideo (:199) is the one-off classifier; all strict guards enumerated in hard_constraints. Wrote the Discovery inbox for product-manager at .state/inbox/product-manager.md (turn the 5 into grouped, tagged ACs; note which need PE design: 1,2,4,5; item 3 is more direct but rides through Design and depends on item 2). Surfaced 5 forks for Dean with recommendations: item 2 order-source = current home sort (rec) + disable-at-ends (rec); item 3 pref storage = db.settings defaultView-style (rec); item 4 restore = sessionStorage view+scroll (rec); item 5 = extract-then-strictly-validate (rec). Set artifacts.exec_plan to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md (PM to author). Advancing bootstrap -> discovery; routing to PM; then /prep-pe-design.

PRINCIPAL-ENGINEER completed Design (## Design section in the exec plan): FR-1 persistent-shell + vanilla SPA-lite fetch-and-swap router (each of the four pages keeps its full server-rendered document as the progressive-enhancement fallback, wraps per-view content in #view-root; per-page scripts become registered init/destroy view modules; the <video> is hoisted into one persistent player host cloned from a shell <template>, reparented between inline #player-slot (FULL) and fixed #player-dock (DOCKED) with an iOS reparent resume-guard + documented fixed-overlay fallback; player features preserved by a persistent player controller player.js split from the watch view). FR-2 shared deriveOrderedIds/computeNeighbors re-derived on the watch view; FR-3 server autoplayNext read by the controller's 'ended' handler; FR-4 retains the timer-free home view node + scroll; FR-5 trim/extract-first-URL pre-step in validateChannelUrl with every strict guard unchanged. PE proposed a T1-T5 breakdown (T1/T2/T5 heavier two-reviewer gate).

ENGINEERING-MANAGER finalized the ## Task breakdown (T1-T5) and confirmed the PE's proposed ordering. DEPENDENCY GRAPH: T5 is INDEPENDENT (isolated to lib/ytdlp/url.js, zero client surface) -> starts first / in PARALLEL. T1 -> T2 -> T3 -> T4 is the FR-1-rooted chain: T2 depends on T1 (needs the shell's #player-slot/#player-dock/#view-root + router transition hook to reparent into); T3 depends on T1 AND T2 (FR-2 prev/next controls live in T1's watch view module; FR-3 autoplay-next fires from T2's controller 'ended' handler; the pure deriveOrderedIds/computeNeighbors helpers may be authored early but wiring needs T1+T2); T4 depends on T1 only (router viewCache hook + home view-node retention) and may run alongside T3 once T1 lands. REVIEW GATES: HEAVIER two-reviewer/adversarial for T1, T2, T5; LIGHTER single-QA no-regression for T3, T4; Dean's on-device pass is the arbiter for all playback/mobile/nav UX (esp. smooth-no-reload feel + iOS Safari reparent). Recorded per-task scope/files/ACs/gate in the exec plan and the tasks array. Set artifacts.requirements + artifacts.design to the exec plan path. Advancing discovery/tasks -> IMPLEMENTATION with TWO PARALLEL SDE TRACKS kicked off: Track A = T5 (status in-progress), Track B = T1 (status in-progress, foundation). Wrote inbox files .state/inbox/software-developer-T5.md and .state/inbox/software-developer-T1.md. Remaining T2/T3/T4 stay pending behind T1(/T2).

PRODUCT-MANAGER: mid-Discovery REVISION from Dean -- item 1 (FR-1) is NO LONGER native Picture-in-Picture; Dean changed the decision after seeing the tradeoff. FR-1 is now a docked persistent mini-player backed by a persistent app shell + SPA-lite client-side navigation (fetch+swap content on in-app nav, pushState/popstate for back/forward, full-page-load fallback preserved for fresh/deep-link loads), because 'video keeps playing smoothly across navigation' cannot be met by PiP alone in today's multi-page app -- it requires the <video> element to survive in-app navigation. FR-1 is now the HEAVY architectural item for this round, flagged for the two-reviewer/adversarial gate (same tier as FR-5), given the risk to deep links, back-button, and scroll. FR-4 (persist home-view state) is RECONCILED: no longer a standalone item needing its own persistence mechanism (sessionStorage/history.state) -- it now rides on FR-1's persistent shell and is reduced to a thin restore-on-return + clean-on-fresh-load acceptance requirement. FR-2, FR-3, FR-5 are UNCHANGED (same ACs as originally scoped). Authored/rewrote docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md (the file did not yet exist -- authored fresh, incorporating both the original Discovery inbox instructions and this revision) with Goal/Scope/Out-of-scope/Constraints/Product-forks/grouped-tagged-Acceptance-criteria (FR-1 x12 ACs, FR-2 x4, FR-3 x5+dependency-note, FR-4 x2+note, FR-5 x7+process, Cross-cutting x7 = 37 criteria total)/Design-flags-for-PE/Decision-log. Two NEW product forks surfaced by the pivot, both resolved with a recommendation (not blocking): (a) which views become SPA-swappable -- recommend all four main in-shell views (home, watch, setup, subscriptions-when-enabled) for a uniform shell rather than special-casing; (b) whether the docked player persists on setup/subscriptions too -- recommend yes, it docks there too, since those are now in-shell views and NOT docking there would silently violate the 'keep playing across navigation' hard requirement. Reported back to EM; did NOT advance stage -- EM to verify the plan and run /prep-pe-design, noting FR-1 as the heavy PE-design item requiring the two-reviewer gate at review time.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Acceptance: read the exec plan's 37 tagged ACs (FR-1 x12, FR-2 x4, FR-3 x5+dependency, FR-4 x2+note, FR-5 x7+process, cross-cutting x7) and verified each directly against the delivered code (all 4 shells, common.js's router, player.js, watch.js, main.js, setup.js, subscriptions.js, server.js's autoplayNext settings, lib/ytdlp/url.js) and the test suite (router-helpers.test.js, player-state.test.js, prev-next.test.js, ytdlp-url.test.js, autoplay-next-api.test.js). Confirmed: the release-blocking pushState-before-swap fix is in place and documented in common.js's navigate() comment; all 7 mandated FR-5 security/acceptance cases present and matching the reconciled AC#6; FR-2/FR-3 share the exact same deriveOrderedIds/computeNeighbors helpers (no divergence); FR-4's homeViewCache is purely in-memory (never leaks into a fresh load); all new CSS uses era tokens (one pre-existing-pattern exception for the dock's overlay close button, consistent with the existing .skip-btn convention); no new deps; textContent-only for new dynamic strings. 16 UNIT/INTEGRATION/PROCESS ACs PASS outright; 21 MANUAL ACs are code-verified with Dean's on-device pass reserved as the documented arbiter for feel/UX (per this round's own process_plan -- not a gap). No AC found genuinely undelivered. Verdict: ACCEPT-WITH-NOTES. Wrote the '## Acceptance' section into docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md with the full per-FR breakdown. Advanced stage implementation -> acceptance-complete; did not commit/push (git is coordinator-owned) and made no code changes. Recommends advancing to Done once Dean's on-device pass confirms the MANUAL items.
